label: Self-consistency & best-of-N inference

node.label: Self-consistency & best-of-N inference
node.definition: Self-consistency samples multiple reasoning trajectories and aggregates their answers, using diversity to reduce dependence on one brittle chain.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Self-consistency & best-of-N inference is to connect Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning, Recursive partitioning & ensembling to downstream capabilities including Reflection, critique, verifier & repair loops, Test-time compute scaling, Best-of-N & verifier-guided selection.
examples: substrate for Reflection, critique, verifier & repair loops
limitations: Search and self-evaluation can multiply cost while preserving confident errors when states, tools, or verifiers are weak. Its direct dependencies include Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning, Recursive partitioning & ensembling.

dependencies.rationale: Self-consistency & best-of-N inference uses Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Self-consistency & best-of-N inference uses Recursive partitioning & ensembling as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Self-consistency & best-of-N inference uses Search, sampling & decoding procedures as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
